principles: Choose the smallest data type that can hold all possible values
Use appropriate numeric types (SMALLINT, INTEGER, BIGINT) based on value range
Use VARCHAR with appropriate length limits instead of TEXT when length is known
Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE for timezone-aware timestamps
Use BOOLEAN instead of INTEGER for true/false values
Use ENUM types for fixed sets of values (but consider maintenance overhead)
Use JSON/JSONB for semi-structured data when appropriate
Consider storage and performance implications of data type choices
commonMistakes: Using VARCHAR(255) for all text fields without considering actual needs
Using INTEGER for boolean values (use BOOLEAN)
Using TEXT for all string fields (use VARCHAR with appropriate length)
Not considering timezone requirements for timestamp fields
Using FLOAT/DOUBLE for monetary values (use NUMERIC/DECIMAL)
Using overly large numeric types when smaller ones would suffice
performanceImpact: Smaller data types reduce storage and improve cache efficiency
Appropriate data types enable better index usage
Correct data types prevent implicit casting in queries
Proper types improve query planning and execution
